posts.author: alice (alice)
posts.cookedHtml:
When creating charts, it would be really helpful if the value on the x-axis is visible even if the Y-value is zero.

For example, if I have this data regarding responses to an invite :

  • yes = 5 people
  • maybe = 7 people
  • no = o people

and I am creating a bar chart and the y-axis is number of people and the x-axis is response.

At the moment, the x-axis will only state ‘yes’ and ‘maybe’. 'No 'won’t be on the x-axis as it has no values (i.e. no one has responded no yet)

This is really frustraiting - we always need to know across lots of different reports we have if the ‘y-value’ is 0.

This means that instead of creating reports in Monday, I need to download the data and create graphs in excel. the excel graphs are not live so they are only accurate for a short period of time which means i have to do this over and over agai.

it would be very very very helpful if you could add this function - thank you!

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml: upvote! (I am out and they aren’t resetting each month any more for some reason)
